dataset: NOAA OISST V2 and NCEP-NCAR Reanalysis 1

pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicit enforcement and dataset extraction
setup_task.sh and export_result.sh explicitly use this directory
setup_task.sh and export_result.sh verify these exact files
setup_task.sh instructions specify 'August (Time Index 7)' which corresponds to the 8th month in the 12-month LTM netcdf time array
setup_task.sh sets the region as 'Persian Gulf basin (~23°N-31°N, 47°E-57°E)'

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real long-term mean climatology datasets (NOAA Optimum Interpolation SST V2 and NCEP-NCAR Reanalysis 1). Programmatic extraction of the data in the specified Persian Gulf bounding box (~23°N-31°N, 47°E-57°E) for the month of August (time index 7) reveals the actual peak sea surface temperature (SST) is approximately 34.0°C and the peak surface air temperature is approximately 39.5°C. Based on the task's heat risk classification guidance, these values trigger the EXTREME heat risk level classification (SST > 32.0°C and Air > 35.0°C).
